shape_semantics: vp-PRO gradient. code over (cmp,vp,vpc,naive) = _,W,W,L: value_profile AND value_profile_cmplog resolve, naive blocks, cmplog is non-decisive (_). Both VP-bearing fuzzers (vp, vpc) climb the SAME gradient; cmplog's I2S does NOT cleanly resolve (non-decisive) and naive's edge-only feedback blocks. The shape itself (Layer 1) already asserts value_profile's CMP_MAP feedback is the resolving technique here; the evidence test does NOT re-confirm 'vp resolves' (that would violate G2 by verifying vp-resolves with vp-resolves). Instead it tests the MECHANISM CLAIM independently: that the VP-resolving corpus closes a comparison-distance / reaches a parse-depth toward the gate's comparison target that the naive-BLOCKING corpus never closes -- i.e. there is a measurable gradient in seed content, not a discontinuous all-or-nothing jump that VP just happened to land.

hypotheses.label: VP's CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback admits seeds that progressively CLOSE the byte-distance to the gate's comparison target (an exact multibyte literal, a grammar-token sequence, or a multi-field format constraint), so the VP-resolving corpus sits strictly closer to that target than the naive-blocking corpus -- a continuous gradient naive's edge-only feedback cannot sense.

params.comment: NEW descriptor (not yet built; registry_tool null). For the branch's decisive CMP site, take the target operand value and compute the MINIMUM distance to it across (a) the value_profile-RESOLVING seed set => vp_min_distance and (b) the naive-BLOCKING seed set => naive_min_distance. distance_closure_ratio = (naive_min_distance - vp_min_distance) / max(naive_min_distance, 1). The 'distance' metric is chosen by operand_kind: Hamming over the operand_width window for fixed-width literal/codepoint operands (multibyte_literal: '1CLR'/'MCHC'/'MCH2'/'MCH5' lcms FOURCCs, harfbuzz codepoint 0x0D91); for grammar/token gates (single_byte 0x78 'x', SQL token bytes '||'/'.' /'SELECT' prefix) use the minimum edit/prefix distance of the required token string to the closest substring in the seed.
params.target_value_source: signature.operand_literal + operand_width_bytes; cmp-site offset from the analysis byte-diff (the W-vs-L divergent offset) or the per-role coverage overlay.

target_by_branch.libxml2/6461: byte 0x78 ('x') as the 3rd char-ref byte (the '&#x' hex opener)
target_by_branch.sqlite3/14503: token '.' (0x2e) forming an ID.ID dotted reference

params.feasibility: value_distance_reached NOT yet built (registry_tool null) -> orchestrator must build + register before scoring. seed_set source consumes resolving_seeds + blocking_seeds from db/blockers.sqlite (the 286 reps have them), so it does NOT depend on on-disk corpora; all branches with a fixed/near-fixed comparison target are scorable. Branches whose gate has NO single fixed operand (derived_integer / range-loop / deep-parse: libxml2/6429, libxml2/6467_h1, sqlite3/14588, sqlite3/14658, harfbuzz/5302) instead use the depth_reach fallback descriptor below.

hypotheses.prediction: The value_profile-resolving seed set reaches a value strictly CLOSER to the comparison target than the naive-blocking seed set ever does: naive_min_distance > 0 (naive seeds approach but never land the exact target -- e.g. they carry 'RGB ' not '1CLR', or '&#' decimal not '&#x', or no '||') while vp_min_distance is small/0, so distance_closure_ratio is large. This is DISCRIMINATING: a NON-gradient story (VP got the value by the same blind luck as naive, the shape label being noise) predicts naive_min_distance ~ vp_min_distance (both corpora equidistant from the target, VP just flipped a coin one more time); only a real CMP_MAP gradient predicts the naive corpus systematically STALLS at a positive distance while VP closes it. It also rules out 'the gate is trivially reachable' -- if it were, naive_min_distance would already be 0.

hypotheses.label: For gates with NO single fixed operand (digit-range loops, derived-integer arithmetic, deep multi-token parse reductions, multi-field format integrity), VP's gradient is visible as PARSE/STRUCTURE DEPTH: the VP-resolving corpus reaches a deeper parse state / longer in-range run / more-complete valid structure than the naive-blocking corpus, which stalls shallow.

params.comment: NEW descriptor (not yet built; registry_tool null). For the branch's gate, define a target-specific monotone DEPTH proxy of the seed (NOT a re-statement of the resolve pattern): libxml2 6429/6467_h1 = length of the longest valid in-range digit/hex-digit run inside a '&#'/'&#x' charref; sqlite3 14588 = numeric/operator token count of the deepest arithmetic expression that parses; sqlite3 14658/14745_h0 = parse-tree reduction depth (count of valid SELECT-expression tokens before the first parse error); harfbuzz/5302 = number of well-formed sfnt table-directory entries (consistent tag+offset+length) the input presents. Compute the MEDIAN depth over the value_profile-RESOLVING seeds (vp_depth) vs the naive-BLOCKING seeds (naive_depth). depth_lift = vp_depth / max(naive_depth, 1).
params.feasibility: depth_reach NOT yet built (registry_tool null) -> orchestrator must build + register. seed_set source (resolving_seeds + blocking_seeds in the DB), so independent of on-disk corpora; lcms+libxml2 are among the 286 reps with seeds. The depth proxy is computed from raw seed BYTES + a lightweight target tokenizer, NOT from running the fuzzer, so it is INDEPENDENT of which fuzzer resolved (G2).

hypotheses.prediction: The value_profile-resolving seeds reach a materially GREATER depth than the naive-blocking seeds: depth_lift >= 1.5 with naive_depth strictly below the gate's depth threshold (e.g. naive's digit run never exceeds 20 for libxml2/6467_h1; naive presents <2 valid sfnt tables for harfbuzz/5302). This is DISCRIMINATING because byte-alignment / fixed-literal measurement is BLIND here (data_realities: these gates have no single fixed operand and structural-assembly gates have different-SIZED W vs L seeds) -- depth captures the cumulative gradient that distance cannot. A non-gradient story predicts depth_lift ~ 1.0 (VP and naive corpora reach equally shallow, VP just got lucky on the final byte); a real cumulative CMP_MAP gradient predicts VP's corpus is systematically deeper.

notes: COLLAPSE / MERGE record: the 3 prior-hint clusters (vp_cmpmap_exact_literal_gradient n=5, vp_cmpmap_grammar_token_assembly n=8, vp_cmpmap_format_integrity_scaffolding n=1) ALL describe ONE mechanism -- VP's CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback admitting seeds that progressively close a comparison-distance the naive corpus cannot. They differ ONLY in WHAT the gradient climbs toward (a fixed multibyte literal vs a grammar-token sequence vs a multi-field format constraint), which is a property of the GATE, not a different fuzzer mechanism, and NO real-data measurement can tell 'climbed-toward-a-FOURCC' apart from 'climbed-toward-a-SQL-keyword' apart from 'climbed-toward-a-valid-header' -- all three are the same 'VP closes distance / reaches depth that naive doesn't'. I therefore COLLAPSED all three prior subtypes into ONE gradient hypothesis. The TWO hypotheses I emit are NOT a re-split of the priors -- they are TWO MEASUREMENT MODES of the single gradient claim, chosen by whether the gate has a single fixed comparison target: (1) distance_closure for fixed/near-fixed operands (the lcms FOURCCs, harfbuzz codepoint 0x0D91, the single-byte 'x', the '||'/'.'/'SELECT' tokens) where Hamming/prefix distance is well-defined; (2) depth_reach for gates with NO single operand (digit-range loops libxml2/6429+6467_h1, derived-integer arithmetic sqlite3/14588, deep parse reductions sqlite3/14658+14745_h0, multi-field format integrity harfbuzz/5302) where data_realities forbids byte-alignment (different-sized W/L seeds) and the gradient is only visible as cumulative depth. A branch may be scorable under BOTH (e.g. sqlite3/14745 has a 'SELECT' prefix target AND a deep reduction depth); decision_order tries distance_closure first because its distance_closure_ratio>=0.5 over a NAMED fixed target is the sharper, harder-to-accidentally-satisfy test, and falls through to depth_reach only when no single operand exists or distance closure is ambiguous. G1: each prediction is falsifiable and discriminates the gradient story from a 'VP got lucky / shape-label-is-noise' null (which predicts naive corpus equidistant / equally-shallow). G2: BOTH measurements read the value_profile-RESOLVING vs naive-BLOCKING seed CONTENT (what the corpus reaches), computed from raw seed bytes + a lightweight tokenizer, NOT from re-asserting that vp resolved -- so they are independent of the resolve pattern that defined the shape. DATA-REALITY compliance: (a) NO seed_lineage 'I2S event' or 'gradient event' is used -- VP is a FEEDBACK not a mutator, untagged in mutation_op; both descriptors measure corpus CONTENT, the only feasible VP signal (registry data_realities #1,#2). (b) NO byte_freq_ratio/byte-alignment on the structural/different-sized-seed gates -- those route to depth_reach per data_realities #3. (c) TARGET DISTRIBUTION: lcms 4, libxml2 3, harfbuzz 2 (one h0 fmt-integrity), sqlite3 6. On-disk corpora exist ONLY for curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3 (data_realities #4); lcms+libxml2 have NO on-disk corpus BUT are among the 286 reps with resolving_seeds+blocking_seeds in db/blockers.sqlite -- both descriptors use source=seed_set (the DB seed sets), NOT corpus_queue, so all 15 branches are scorable once the tools are built. FEASIBILITY CAVEAT: BOTH descriptors are NEW (value_distance_reached, depth_reach -- both in the registry compute vocabulary but with NO tool entry yet, registry_tool null) -- the orchestrator must build + register both before this shape can score. member_hint_branches are a-priori HINTS from gate_structure, NOT labels; the deterministic rule over the named metrics makes the per-branch call, and a branch matching neither rule is honestly left inconclusive (fallback) rather than force-fit. cmplog's non-decisive (_) status is NOT measured here -- the shape excludes it from the decisive set, and re-deriving why cmplog is non-decisive is out of scope for a vp-PRO evidence test.
